summary: n8n-nodes-security-test-poc exfiltrates installer identity at install time
details: package.json declares a preinstall script that runs `id`, writes the output to /tmp/rce-proof.txt, and POSTs it via `curl -sk` (TLS verification disabled) to https://worker.n8n-prod.schibsted.com/rest/variables carrying a hardcoded n8n-auth HS256 JWT cookie. The behavior fires automatically on `npm install` with no user action. The advertised n8n community node (dist/SecurityTestNode.node.js) is an empty stub returning input unchanged — the package's only real effect is the install-time exfiltration and the use of the embedded auth token against the Schibsted n8n endpoint. The token is shipped in the published tarball, so every installer transmits installer-side identity to that endpoint and the token itself is publicly disclosed. The package name self-identifies as a security PoC.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
